name: mutation
summary: Action-keyed handler that processes a named mutation, dispatched by action string.
description: Creates a mutation handler and registers it in an initializer
problem: You have a system that takes action commands (create, update, archive, refund) by name, and you want each command to live in its own class — discoverable, testable, with no giant dispatch switch.
appliesWhen: Your system processes named state-change actions (often from queues or external commands)
Each action has its own validation and side effects
You want a registry of actions you can introspect and extend
The action set is mostly stable across the lifetime of the feature
avoidWhen: The action is an HTTP request — use phpnomad/controller
It's a one-off CLI script — use phpnomad/command
The dispatching system isn't named-action-based — write a plain service
examples: Handle the refund action via a mutation
Add a mutation for archiving subscriptions
tradeoffs: One handler per action means a lot of files in busy systems, but each one is small and testable.
outputs: A {name} mutation handler class registered for action '{action}'
A registration in the initializer's getMutations() list
postApply: Implement the handler's process method with the actual logic
Decide if this mutation should fire an event when it completes (often: yes)
Wire validation if the action takes structured input

name.aiHint: Verb-noun PascalCase ending in 'Mutation'. The class name describes what it does (CreateUserMutation, ArchiveSubscriptionMutation).

action.description: Action name this mutation handles (e.g. create)

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register in
initializer.aiHint: Pick the initializer that owns related mutations for this domain.
